hypothesis: If supervised fine-tuned language models are further trained with Direct Preference Optimization using thermodynamics-based hierarchical reward feedback from Thermo-Calc, then the resulting DPO models will generate BCC/B2 alloy compositions with higher thermodynamic reward scores, greater compositional validity, coverage, novelty, and unique-pair generation rates than the original SFT models and baseline methods, because preference learning aligns model outputs with physics-based synthesis objectives.
